text:
government affairs, commercial behaviors, social activities, and judicial affairs. In
2012, the Ministerial-Joint Meeting System extended its membership to 35 central
departments, including the Publicity Department (i.e., China’s State Propaganda
Department), Ministry of Finance, and Ministry of Justice.
    Admittedly, much of the early motives and efforts were limited to improve
accounting and loan-lending activities for commerce and finance rather than the
social sectors (Zhang, 2016). The main goal for building a credit system was to
address financial aspects of credit. Another notable feature is that though China
had tried to construct data platforms for credit system, it could not obtain enough
data for credit scoring. The government had limited data to evaluate the economic
and financial domains, and this initial project did not cover the whole of society.

Upgrading of the SCS Since 2014
